name: DLNA Renderer
author: Ava
description: DLNA MediaRenderer for LAN music casting from BubbleUPnP, foobar2000, and other UPnP apps.
detail_description: Turn Ava into a DLNA MediaRenderer:1 speaker with optional Cinema full-screen player and on-screen controls. Supports preemptive casting without Music Assistant, voice ducking, DLNA volume control, Home Assistant diagnostic entities, and autostart on boot.

config.label: Device Name
config.description: Friendly name shown in DLNA controller apps.
config.dialogHint: The name controllers like BubbleUPnP display in their renderer list.

config.label: Auto Start
config.description: Start the DLNA renderer when this mod is enabled.

config.label: Allow Volume Control
config.description: Let DLNA controllers change the device media volume.

config.label: Voice Ducking
config.description: Lower DLNA playback volume while talking to the voice assistant.

config.label: Cinema Overlay
config.description: Show full-screen Cinema player UI while DLNA audio or video is playing.

config.label: Keep Playback Bar Visible
config.description: Keep the bottom playback bar (progress, shuffle/prev/play/next/repeat) always on screen. Only the top back-button header auto-hides. Turn off to auto-hide the bottom bar too.

config.label: Dual Output (Speaker + Earpiece)
config.description: Best-effort route hint for phone-class devices. Default OFF. When enabled, playback tries to drive both loudspeaker and earpiece; actual effect depends on device audio HAL.

config.label: Show Diagnostic Entities
config.description: Expose renderer status entities in Home Assistant.
